title: ferula lutea var. microcarpa
intro: Ferula lutea var. microcarpa, also known as the small-fruited ferula, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as supporting digestive health and reducing pain and inflammation. Ferula lutea var. microcarpa is commonly prepared as an herbal decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat various health conditions due to its bioactive compounds.
